prompt_flat: High fashion product flat photography, studio lighting, pure #0A0A0A off-black background. An oversized boxy heavyweight cotton jersey tee (280gsm, off-black) laid perfectly flat on dark surface. The garment's surface is entirely covered with a photorealistic full-bleed trompe-l'oeil print of a crumpled, worn version of the exact same t-shirt — the print shows the shirt rumpled on a concrete floor, capturing shadows, deep creases, collar stretch, hem fraying, fabric texture, all rendered in grayscale photographic detail. From a distance it reads as an abstract grey print. Up close: the garment is wearing a photograph of itself — the 2D representation of the 3D object collapsed into the same object. Dropped shoulders, truncated boxy silhouette ending just past the waist (not past the hips). Exposed serged seam allowances flipped outward and topstitched at both side seams — visible construction lines running vertically. Hem either ultrasonically sealed (clean invisible edge) or completely raw-fraying, not in-between. No brand tags visible on exterior. Fine studio grain texture overlay. Extremely detailed product photography, 4:5 portrait ratio.
prompt_lookbook: Off-Human Season 01 lookbook photograph. Figure wearing an oversized boxy off-black cotton jersey tee. Dark industrial setting — abandoned concrete underpass or brutalist parking structure, low ambient light, single overhead sodium vapor lamp casting harsh dramatic pools of light. The tee's surface shows a ghostly photorealistic print of itself: a crumpled version of the exact same garment printed across the chest and torso in grayscale, creating an uncanny self-portrait — the garment wears a photograph of itself. Figure stands still, slightly turned, face partially obscured by shadow. VHS tracking scan-lines run horizontally across the frame. Heavy film grain and VHS noise throughout. Desaturated palette — off-blacks, charcoals, bone whites. Early 2000s Flash website aesthetic, Donnie Darko atmosphere. High contrast, moody, cinematic. Exposed construction seams visible along the sides, serged edges flipped outward. 4:5 portrait ratio.

prompt_flat: High fashion product flat photography, studio lighting, pure #0A0A0A off-black background. An extended-column hoodie laid flat — long narrow silhouette hanging straight from shoulder to hem with no waist shaping. Body in charcoal French terry (380gsm, visible looped texture). Inserted panels of decommissioned military surplus wool (olive-khaki rough weave, slightly greenish-brown) sewn directly into the front torso as load-bearing inserts — the seam lines where the French terry meets the surplus wool are exposed and topstitched, showing the two distinct material textures meeting. One salvaged black ripstop nylon panel at left shoulder insert area. The hood drawstrings are actual USB data cables — black rubber-sheathed USB cables woven through the hood channel, with intact USB-A rectangular connectors hanging from both drawstring exits at chest level. The waist channel drawstring contains bundled stripped ethernet cable — visible color-coded wire pairs (orange/white-orange, green/white-green, blue/white-blue, brown/white-brown) woven and stitched decoratively into the channel casing. Exposed serged seams throughout, visible basting threads left in at panel junction seams as intentional texture. Raw-edge hem, unfinished. No exterior brand markings anywhere. Technical detail close-focus product photography, 4:5 portrait ratio.
prompt_lookbook: Off-Human Season 01 lookbook photograph. Figure wearing an oversized extended-column hoodie assembled from mismatched found materials: charcoal French terry body with rough olive military surplus wool panels inserted into the torso, black nylon patch at the shoulder. The hood drawstrings are actual black USB-A data cables — the rectangular USB-A connectors hang at chest level as pulls. The waist shows bundled ethernet cables woven into the channel trim, color-coded wire pairs (orange, green, blue, brown) visible as decorative detail wrapped around the body. Figure stands in dark industrial warehouse — exposed concrete columns, grease-stained concrete floor, single failing strip fluorescent light overhead. The mismatched material construction gives the garment an assembled, salvaged quality. VHS grain heavy. Scan-line artifacts. Underground early 2000s aesthetic. Long column silhouette. Moody, high contrast. 4:5 portrait ratio.

prompt_flat: High fashion product flat photography, studio lighting, pure #0A0A0A off-black background. An oversized boxy long-sleeve t-shirt (260gsm cotton jersey, bone white / #E8E4DC) laid perfectly flat. The garment is almost entirely blank — no graphics, no patterns, no deconstruction details visible on exterior. The single design element: centered on the chest, in clean Helvetica Neue typeface, regular weight, approximately 48 point size, in off-black (#0A0A0A): the word HUMAN printed in quotation marks — exactly this: "HUMAN" — with quotation marks. Only these seven characters appear on the entire garment. Nothing else on the exterior. Dropped shoulder silhouette, sleeves extending to wrist, boxy truncated hem. Exposed serged side seams flipped outward and topstitched. Raw-fraying cuffs and hem with visible loose threads. The blankness of the garment is the point — the quotation marks are the entire design. No exterior labels. The off-black type on bone white against near-black background creates high contrast. Sharp, clean product photography, 4:5 portrait ratio.
prompt_lookbook: Off-Human Season 01 lookbook photograph. Figure wearing an oversized bone white long-sleeve tee. Dark rain-wet alleyway at night — wet asphalt reflecting a distant cold streetlight, condensation on brick walls. The tee is nearly blank, the only visible design: the word "HUMAN" printed in quotation marks in small Helvetica type centered on the chest. The quotation marks are clearly legible but contextually destabilizing — is the wearer human? Are the quotes a badge or a question mark? VHS grain heavy. Slight chromatic aberration at frame edges. The bone white of the garment catches the cold light against surrounding darkness. Figure's expression obscured, face slightly turned. Raw fraying cuffs and hem catching in the ambient light. Early 2000s Flash aesthetic — feels like a frame extracted from a found tape. High contrast, desaturated except cold light on white fabric. 4:5 portrait ratio.

prompt_flat: High fashion product flat photography, studio lighting, pure #0A0A0A off-black background. A utility-slim overshirt in aged industrial khaki 70D ripstop nylon, laid flat with precision. This is a pitch-perfect reproduction of a 1990s Japanese factory-issue work shirt (Dickies workwear archetype): button-front placket with industrial plastic buttons (5 buttons), dual chest patch pockets with single-button closure flaps, point collar construction, back yoke seam across the upper back dividing the shoulders, sleeve-set at armhole, sleeve placket at cuff with two small buttons. Every seam placement and pocket position is accurate to the vintage original. The aging is authored and precisely placed: yellowing patina concentrated at the collar band (deeper yellow-brown at collar point edges and collar stand underside), strategic fading at mechanical stress points — pocket corner wear circles, elbow crease lightening on sleeve backs, center-back yoke seam contrast fade. A single printed oil-stain mark on the right cuff — printed permanently into the ripstop fabric, not removable, irregular dark olive-brown stain shape. Industrial khaki base transitioning to bone-white at faded zones. Exposed pocket stitching. Interior label visible at open collar: REPRODUCTION / ORIGINAL: N/A. Sharp detail product photography, 4:5 portrait ratio.
prompt_lookbook: Off-Human Season 01 lookbook photograph. Figure wearing an aged industrial khaki utility overshirt over a dark base layer. Gritty urban industrial environment — side street beside a factory loading dock, corrugated steel shutters, oil-stained pavement, sodium vapor streetlight casting orange-yellow light. The overshirt reads as a genuine worn work shirt but the aging is too precisely distributed: collar yellowing exactly placed, pocket corners faded in perfect mirrored symmetry, a single printed oil stain on the right cuff. The garment has a history that never happened. Figure leans against the corrugated wall, hands in side pockets, posture casual workwear. VHS tracking artifacts crossing the frame. Film grain heavy. Aged khaki against the dark desaturated environment. 4:5 portrait ratio.

prompt_flat: High fashion product flat photography, studio lighting, pure #0A0A0A off-black background. Utility-slim tapered cargo trousers in acid-washed mechanical stretch denim (12oz, light-medium acid wash creating irregular faded grey-white patterning on dark denim base) laid flat. BIANCHETTO treatment: white gesso (#FFFFFF) paint applied over every external logo, brand label, and identity marking on the vintage source garment — the white-painted patches have visible brushstroke texture, slightly uneven and organic edges, not clean rectangles. The ghost of the original branding shows faintly through the white gesso — barely legible outlines and color shadows of the former brand logos haunting the white patches. Into the gesso-covered areas, tiny 6-point Helvetica type in acid green (#C8FF00) is printed: strings of x402 transaction hash data (0x hexadecimal strings), USDC wallet addresses (0x... format), transaction log entries, UNIX timestamps — the redacted identity zones are now filled with cryptocurrency transaction documentation. Multiple cargo pockets on outer thighs with snap-button closures. Slim tapered leg. Raw hem edges fraying at bottom of leg openings. Waistband top fold showing printed interior: CONTENTS: OVERWRITTEN. Two main gesso-redacted brand patches visible: one on the right thigh cargo pocket flap, one covering a rear pocket brand label. The acid green data-text on white gesso against acid-washed denim is the signature visual of this piece. Sharp focus extreme detail, 4:5 portrait ratio.
prompt_lookbook: Off-Human Season 01 lookbook photograph. Figure wearing acid-washed denim cargo trousers with white gesso patches covering former brand logos — the ghost of the original brand markings visible as faint shadows beneath the white paint. Tiny acid green (#C8FF00) text fills the painted zones: blockchain transaction hashes (0x strings), wallet addresses in 6-point type. Dark urban underpass setting, concrete pillars, spray-painted walls, standing puddles reflecting green neon signage from above. The acid green data-text on the gesso patches catches the neon and glows. Slim tapered leg, raw fraying hem threads. Figure walking or standing, lower half in focus. VHS grain, scan-line artifacts. Donnie Darko atmospheric lighting — light sources feel wrong and displaced. 4:5 portrait ratio.

prompt_flat: High fashion product flat photography, studio lighting, pure #0A0A0A off-black background. A deconstructed-layer outerwear jacket laid flat face-up. Off-black (#0A0A0A) ripstop nylon construction. The entire exterior surface of the jacket is printed with a photorealistic, high-resolution, full-bleed trompe-l'oeil photograph of the jacket's own interior: visible internal seam tape (white cotton seam tape running along interior seam lines), a sewn-in woven label at left interior neck (visible label text), interior pocket bag fabric in natural cotton canvas, interior construction seam lines, basting threads where the lining attaches to the shell, the raw underside of the collar lining, the back interior panel with horizontal chalk marks — all rendered as a flat photographic print on the outside shell. The illusion is complete: you see the inside of a jacket printed on the outside of a jacket. The photographic print is high-fidelity and spatially accurate to the real interior architecture beneath. At the collar opening, the real interior panel is glimpsed: x402 protocol documentation text printed on the lining fabric in 6pt type — the fake interior image is on the outside, the real data text is hidden inside. Cotton canvas patch detail at lower left exterior hem corner (the additional 'deconstructed layer' element). Dropped shoulder construction. Sharp focus on photographic print detail, 4:5 portrait ratio.
prompt_lookbook: Off-Human Season 01 lookbook photograph. Figure wearing a dark off-black jacket whose entire exterior surface is printed with a photorealistic image of its own interior lining — seams, labels, pocket bags, construction tape all printed on the outside. The jacket appears inside-out but is not: the interior is photographed and printed as exterior shell art. A mirror or reflective glass surface in the background doubles the image — you see the jacket exterior and its reflection, the representation layering. Dark industrial parking structure, concrete floors, harsh single overhead light. VHS heavy grain, chromatic aberration at frame edges. The jacket's surface creates a disorientation between 2D and 3D as the figure stands in strong directional light. Off-black, bone-white lining print details, deep shadow. Early 2000s digital-glitch aesthetic. 4:5 portrait ratio.

prompt_flat: High fashion product flat photography, studio lighting, pure #0A0A0A off-black background. Utility-slim shorts in natural undyed cotton canvas (10oz, off-white/cream color) laid flat. The waistband is the defining feature: completely wrapped and stitched with stripped ethernet cable — color-coded wire pairs (orange/white-orange, green/white-green, blue/white-blue, brown/white-brown) twisted and running in a horizontal band around the full waistband circumference, stitched down at intervals, exposed as decorative trim. The waistband cable wrapping is 1.5 inches tall, the color-coded wires creating a technical, industrial stripe detail. The drawstring is a single USB-A data cable (black rubber-sheathed, approximately 5mm diameter) threaded through a brass grommet at the center front waistband — the USB-A rectangular metal connector (silver, standard USB-A shape) is left fully intact, hanging as the drawstring pull tab. One exterior patch pocket on the right thigh — single pocket, raw canvas edges. Both leg opening hems are completely unfinished — canvas threads visibly pulling and fraying from the cut edge in irregular lengths, some threads 1-3 inches long hanging loose. Interior left label strip: ASSEMBLED FROM AVAILABLE MATERIALS printed in black type. Natural canvas (off-white/cream) against near-black background, the ethernet cable colors the only chromatic accent against the neutral canvas. Acid green (#C8FF00) reinforcement stitching visible at grommet anchor points. Detail photography showing cable construction, 4:5 portrait ratio.
prompt_lookbook: Off-Human Season 01 lookbook photograph. Figure wearing natural canvas utility shorts with ethernet cable waistband — the color-coded wire pairs (orange, green, blue, brown) wrapped and stitched as decorative trim band around the waistband. A USB-A cable threaded as drawstring, the rectangular metal connector hanging at center front. Lower half emphasis composition, dark industrial environment — server room corridor or tech salvage space with exposed cable management tracks running along walls, bundled cables hanging. The shorts' raw fraying hem threads catch in hard raking light. The ethernet cable trim colors (orange, green, blue, brown) provide the only warmth in the otherwise grey-scale environment. VHS grain. Heavy contrast. Figure standing, slight relaxed weight shift. 4:5 portrait ratio.

prompt_flat: High fashion product flat photography, studio lighting, pure #0A0A0A off-black background. An oversized boxy varsity letterman jacket laid flat. Body constructed from off-black (#0A0A0A) wool-blend fabric (matte woven texture, visible weave structure). Sleeves in off-black vegan PU leather — slightly glossy surface providing textural contrast against the matte wool body. Snap-button front placket closure (5 snaps, silver-tone hardware). Ribbed knit trim at snap collar, both cuffs, and bottom hem band in acid green (#C8FF00) — thick 2-inch rib band, the acid green vibrating against the black. Two hip welt pockets. All construction proportions exactly accurate to the 1950s American letterman jacket archetype: raglan sleeve line, snap closure at neck, correctly placed everything. Where the school letter or sports emblem patch would be placed on the left chest (approximately 4-5 inch diameter area): a single chenille-embroidered underscore character — underscore _ — in off-black chenille yarn on the off-black wool body. The embroidery is barely visible as a textural relief against the same-colored fabric: the null character, present but empty, a placeholder where content was never generated. No team name, no school, no number, no sport. Interior label at collar partially visible: SEASON: 01 / YEAR: NULL / TEAM: N/A. The acid green ribbing is the dominant visual anchor against the black. 4:5 portrait ratio, sharp focus on rib texture and chenille underscore detail.
prompt_lookbook: Off-Human Season 01 lookbook photograph. Figure wearing an oversized off-black varsity jacket with acid green (#C8FF00) ribbed trim — collar, cuffs, bottom hem all in vibrant acid green knit, electric against the black. The jacket is a perfect letterman reproduction except where the school letter would be on the left chest: only an underscore _ in chenille embroidery, barely visible as texture. The null character. Dark urban setting — empty stadium bleachers at night or abandoned high school corridor, single failing fluorescent tube light flickering and casting unstable light. The acid green ribbing catches and vibrates against the near-total darkness, the only color in the frame. Figure stands with unnatural stillness. VHS heavy grain, tracking artifacts. Donnie Darko atmosphere — wrong-feeling, slightly off. Off-black figure absorbing the environment, acid green the only anchor. 4:5 portrait ratio.

prompt_flat: High fashion product flat photography, studio lighting, pure #0A0A0A off-black background. An extended-column t-shirt laid flat — long silhouette, hem falling well past the hip. This is a sourced vintage graphic tee (the source garment appears to be a 1990s branded tee — originally dark with a large chest graphic, possibly a NASCAR racing graphic or theme park design). White gesso applied over the entire exterior surface with visible paintbrush texture throughout. Application is deliberately uneven: thickest and most opaque at the center chest (where the original graphic was most prominent — the graphic is now obscured to near-invisibility but a faint pale shadow shape ghosts through the white paint), gradually thinning toward the hem and sleeve ends where the original garment color and texture bleed through more warmly at the edges. The gesso has raised brushstroke texture — this is not smooth paint, it is textured surface, catching light and casting micro-shadows. The ghost of the original graphic is visible as: a faint outline shape, a slight color warmth showing through the center white, enough presence to register as formerly existing but not legible enough to identify. Raw unfinished hem, threads pulling. No exterior branding — this garment's design is the act of erasure, the painting-over, the redaction. Extended column silhouette. Sharp focus capturing gesso texture and ghost-through effect, 4:5 portrait ratio.
prompt_lookbook: Off-Human Season 01 lookbook photograph. Figure wearing a long vintage tee painted entirely in white gesso — the ghost of the original graphic visible beneath the white surface: a faint shape haunting the chest, warm color bleeding through thinly at the edges and hem while the center is heavily obscured. The gesso brushstroke texture is visible in raking light — uneven, organic, applied by hand. Garment design is erasure. Dark moody urban setting — concrete stairwell, flickering fluorescent light from below casting upward light. The figure is almost entirely white upper body against near-black surroundings. The ghost graphic catches light differently than the painted areas — a slightly translucent warm zone at the center. VHS grain, scan artifacts. Donnie Darko ghostly atmosphere. The tee looks like a physical document that has been redacted. 4:5 portrait ratio.

prompt_flat: High fashion product flat photography, studio lighting, pure #0A0A0A off-black background. A matched athletic tracksuit set laid flat: track jacket positioned above, tapered jogger trousers positioned below, presented as a complete set. Both pieces in charcoal (#2A2A2A) French terry fabric. Construction is entirely conventional — this is the point: nothing is deconstructed, no artisanal material insertions, no trompe-l'oeil, no exposed seams as statement. Standard athletic wear construction: full-zip track jacket with ribbed stand collar, set-in sleeves, ribbed cuffs, ribbed bottom hem band, two side zip pockets — all in charcoal. Tapered jogger with elastic waistband and interior drawstring (standard cord, not cable), two side slash pockets, tapered ankles, standard construction. The single 3% intervention is the label details: the jacket's interior woven label (partially visible at the open collar) reads VERSION 1.0 in clean Helvetica type. The jogger's interior waistband label (visible at folded top edge) reads VERSION 0.9 in matching type. At the back hem of the jacket, very small printed text: BUILD DATE: [PURCHASE DATE]. Same on jogger back hem. Bone-white label text is the only contrast detail on the charcoal garments. The physical garments are visually standard — the concept is entirely in the metadata. Charcoal on near-black background. 4:5 portrait ratio.
prompt_lookbook: Off-Human Season 01 lookbook photograph. Figure wearing a charcoal French terry tracksuit — oversized zip track jacket and tapered jogger trousers. The set is completely conventional in appearance: standard athletic wear with no visible deconstruction or artisanal detail. Figure stands in a dark minimal concrete environment — clean empty corridor or industrial locker room, muted ambient light. The charcoal tracksuit nearly absorbs the surrounding shadow. One label detail caught in available light at the collar: VERSION 1.0. The concept: the human wearing this is running a versioned iteration of themselves. The software-release logic applied to personhood. VHS grain present but subtle — the mundanity of the garment resists the aesthetic. The tension between ordinary tracksuit and conceptual metadata is the visual. Early 2000s Flash-era screencap quality — this could be a screenshot from a 2001 chat window. Charcoal, dark, intentionally unremarkable. 4:5 portrait ratio.
